ground_truth_facts.fact: User has patellofemoral syndrome in the right knee diagnosed by a doctor
User wants to lose 15 kg, currently weighing 92 kg
User works night shifts from 10pm to 7am
User prefers home workouts over going to the gym
User has already lost 5 kg over the past 3 months

conversation_messages: Hey, I want to start getting serious about fitness. I'm 92kg right now and I really want to lose around 15kg. Where do I even start?
The main issue is my right knee. Doctor told me last year it's patellofemoral syndrome. Some exercises just kill it.
I work night shifts — 10pm to 7am — so my schedule is completely flipped. How do I plan workouts around that?
I don't have a gym membership and honestly don't want one. I'd rather just work out at home if possible.
What kind of diet changes should I make? Also I should mention I'm lactose intolerant so anything with milk or whey protein is out for me.
I've actually been doing okay — lost about 5kg in the last 3 months just by cutting out junk food. But now I feel like I need to add exercise.
My knee acts up mostly during squats and going down stairs. Running is fine as long as it's not too long.
What's the best time for me to work out given my shift? I usually sleep from 8am to 3pm.
Can you suggest a fat loss diet that works for someone lactose intolerant? Dairy alternatives are fine.
I tried doing bodyweight squats yesterday and my knee was not happy. Are there alternatives that don't stress the knee as much?
How many calories should I be eating to lose weight? I'm a software engineer so pretty sedentary during work hours even though it's night shift.
I want to track my progress properly. Should I focus on scale weight or measurements?
Is 15kg weight loss achievable in 6 months or am I being unrealistic?

probe_queries.query: Can I do squats and lunges in my workout?
What protein shake can I have after my workout?
I work nights, when is the best time for me to train?
How far am I from my goal?
Give me a home workout plan for fat loss
My knee is hurting after yesterday's session, what should I do?
